My wife took up vaping after stopping smoking. Before, she only smoked outside or in the garage. She did this because she knew I hated any smoking, and I was grateful. Now she vapes 24/7. It rarely leaves her hand. Its plugged in to charge each night (just like her phone) and always the first thing she picks up. If she goes for a wee in the night I can hear it crackling away. In the car, in the home, it never stops. I cant suggest cutting down as it leads to arguments, but I hate watching her kill herself slowly. I used to buy them for her, but stopped and she now vapes stronger strenghts. The addictive power of a nicotine vape is frightening. As a society I don't think we have woken up to the looming health disaster. Probably 10 or 20 years away sadly.

Young people who take up vaping risk being hooked for life due to withdrawal symptoms that can be worse than quitting cigarettes, doctors warn.

  • share The real reason you're finding it so hard to quit vaping

The other alternative is to lose weight. I had undiagnosed obstructive sleep apnoea back in 2015 and lost my job because of it because I was going to bed at 9PM, getting up at 6AM and still not feeling refreshed. This led me to nodding off at my desk during the afternoons when I was trying to read documents necessary to do my job as an energy regulations compliance manager. I was eventually diagnosed in 2017 and given a CPAP machine, but after losing two back teeth due to the drying effect of continuous air blowing over the machine, I knew I couldn't continue. At the time of diagnosis I was informed that the primary cause was my weight at 5' 8" and 19 stone, I was far too heavy. This was before all of the weight loss drugs, so nothing to do but eat less and move more. Seven months later I had lost 8 stone in weight and the sleep apnoea was completely cleared. Haven't had any problems since and even handed back my CPAP machine for someone else to use.

A prolific parcel thief who stole thousands of pounds' worth of packages faces prison after a woman found he was trying to sell her nicked goods online... and tracked him down using his tattoos.  Camila Martins, from Westminster in London, was left perplexed after receiving a call from a DHL delivery driver on March 12 asking where the package she had left for collection was.  Her confusion deepened after she discovered that another parcel - this one delivered by DPD - was also missing, despite receiving an email to say it had been dropped off at 12.42pm.

The Odyssey first trailer teases Matt Damon's mythical post-war quest while Anne Hathaway's Penelope battles temptation as they join Tom Holland and Zendaya in Christopher Nolan's star-studded epic

The first trailer for Christopher Nolan's much-anticipated film The Odyssey has finally been released, with its star-studded cast already drawing attention ahead of its July release date. The film is set to be an adaptation of Homer's ancient Greek epic the Odyssey, which sees the Greek King embark on a perilous journey home after the Trojan War in a bid to reunite with his beloved wife Penelope. With Matt Damon starring as the titular hero Odysseus, the preview offered a glimpse at the trials and tribulations he'll face on his quest.

Moment police chase ends in carnage as driver smashes into parked cars on quiet residential road

A police chase ended in carnage when a fleeing driver smashed into parked cars on a quiet residential street. Doorbell footage captured the moment the vehicle lost control on Ancona Road in Plumstead, south-east London, in the early hours of Monday - before officers caught the driver and recovered a stash of drugs. The video shows the car careering at high speed into a row of vehicles on the left side of the street, parked outside terraced houses, after police tried to pull it over for erratic driving.

Down to the drivers going too fast or not paying attention not the geography.

Barbara Smith, who runs The Woolpack in the village of Sopley, Dorset, said drivers were consistently caught out by a T-junction followed by a sharp bend around the pub. Police were called shortly before 4am on Monday morning after a silver Renault Megane drove across the junction and ploughed through the brick wall of a historic bridge next to the pub.
  • 27 comments
  • 1 video
  • share Pub landlady calls for signs at T-junction after NINE vehicles crash
Got a story for Daily Mail?

Email

Landowners selling off their inheritances to developers for millions. The end of farmland 😡

Viscount Lymington and his company, Farleigh North Farm, have put forward plans to construct an enormous 378-acre housing development known as 'Upper Swallick' on farmland surrounding the tiny villages of Farleigh Wallop and Cliddesden. The proposal has left residents up in arms who claim the homes, which could virtually connect the area with neighbouring Basingstoke, will destroy their local village. Proposals for Upper Swallick garden village includes 1,000 affordable properties, a primary school, a new village centre with shops, a commercial space, sports pitches and community facilities as well as plans for traveller pitches. A staggering 11,800 people have signed a petition against the planning application submitted by Oliver Wallop - a British peer based in the area.
  • 52 comments
  • share Homeowners' fury over plan to build 2,500-home 'garden village'
